From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American Catholic priest and 18th president of Notre Dame

For other people named Robert Dowd, see [Robert Dowd (disambiguation)](/wiki/Robert_Dowd_(disambiguation) "Robert Dowd (disambiguation)").

**Robert Alfred Dowd Jr.**, [C.S.C.](/wiki/Congregation_of_Holy_Cross "Congregation of Holy Cross") (born 1965)[[1]](#cite_note-1) is an [American Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church_in_the_United_States "Catholic Church in the United States") priest of the [Congregation of Holy Cross](/wiki/Congregation_of_Holy_Cross "Congregation of Holy Cross") and academic who has served as [president](/wiki/President_of_the_University_of_Notre_Dame "President of the University of Notre Dame") of the [University of Notre Dame](/wiki/University_of_Notre_Dame "University of Notre Dame") since 2024.

## Early life and career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_A._Dowd&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and career")]

Dowd was born in 1965, in [Louisville, Kentucky](/wiki/Louisville,_Kentucky "Louisville, Kentucky"), to Robert Alfred Dowd Sr. and Norma Dowd Krentz. He has a sister, Mary, 11 months younger. At age 2, the family moved to [Michigan City, Indiana](/wiki/Michigan_City,_Indiana "Michigan City, Indiana"), where Dowd ultimately attended [Marquette Catholic High School](/wiki/Marquette_Catholic_High_School_(Indiana) "Marquette Catholic High School (Indiana)"). He earned his B.A. in psychology and economics from the University of Notre Dame in 1987, and an M.Div. from the [Jesuit School of Theology at Berkeley](/wiki/Jesuit_School_of_Theology_of_Santa_Clara_University "Jesuit School of Theology of Santa Clara University") in 1993. He was made his [final profession](/wiki/Religious_profession "Religious profession") for the [Congregation of Holy Cross](/wiki/Congregation_of_Holy_Cross "Congregation of Holy Cross") on August 28, 1993, and was ordained as a [Catholic priest](/wiki/Catholic_priest "Catholic priest") on April 9, 1994.[[2]](#cite_note-2)[[3]](#cite_note-bio_Notre_Dame-3) He later earned an M.A. in African Studies from [UCLA](/wiki/University_of_California,_Los_Angeles "University of California, Los Angeles") in 1998, followed by a Ph.D. in political science in 2003.[[4]](#cite_note-Notre_Dame_News-4)

At Notre Dame, Dowd served as vice president and Associate Provost for Interdisciplinary Initiatives, Assistant Provost for Internationalization, Director of the Ford Program in Human Development Studies and Solidarity, and the director of the Notre Dame Millennial Development Initiative in the [Mpigi District](/wiki/Mpigi_District "Mpigi District") in Uganda, an initiative to advance agriculture, education and health initiatives.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Dowd's 2015 book titled *Christianity, Islam, and Liberal Democracy* was praised by many and called "groundbreaking" by reviewer Muhammed Haron.[[6]](#cite_note-6) In it, he makes the case that religious tolerance in Nigeria and other African countries happens because of a variety of religions, rather than in spite of it.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

## Notre Dame presidency

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_A._Dowd&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Notre Dame presidency")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/b/b7/Senator_John_Thune_and_Senator_Todd_Young_with_Notre_Dame_President_Father_Bob_Dowd.jpg/250px-Senator_John_Thune_and_Senator_Todd_Young_with_Notre_Dame_President_Father_Bob_Dowd.jpg)](/wiki/File:Senator_John_Thune_and_Senator_Todd_Young_with_Notre_Dame_President_Father_Bob_Dowd.jpg)

Dowd (center) with senators [John Thune](/wiki/John_Thune "John Thune") and [Todd Young](/wiki/Todd_Young "Todd Young") in 2025

On December 4, 2023, Notre Dame's Board of Trustees announced that Dowd would succeed [John I. Jenkins](/wiki/John_I._Jenkins "John I. Jenkins") as the 18th university president, and began his term in that role on June 1, 2024.[[4]](#cite_note-Notre_Dame_News-4) He was formally inaugurated at the Purcell Pavilion at the [Joyce Center](/wiki/Joyce_Center "Joyce Center") on Friday, September 13, 2024.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

## Publications

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Robert_A._Dowd&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Publications")]

* — (2015). [*Christianity, Islam and Liberal Democracy : Lessons from Sub-Saharan Africa*](https://archive.org/details/christianityisla0000dowd/page/n3/mode/2up). New York: Oxford University Press. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [978-0-19-022522-3](/wiki/Special:BookSources/978-0-19-022522-3 "Special:BookSources/978-0-19-022522-3").
